---
title: "pair_wise_t_tests_education"
format: pdf
---

```{r include=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(arrow)
library(purrr)
library(survey)
library(srvyr)
library(broom)
library(gt)

data <- read_parquet("../data/clean_data.parquet")

glimpse(data)
```

```{r}
pair_t <- data %>% 
  dplyr::filter(age > 18,
         esr != "Not in Labor Force") %>% 
  mutate(education = factor(education,
                            levels = c("Post-bacc professional degree",
                                       "Doctorate degree",
                                       "Master's degree",
                                       "Bachelor's degree",
                                       "Associate's degree",
                                       "Some college",
                                       "Regular high school diploma",
                                       "GED or alternative credential",
                                       "Less than High School",
                                       "No schooling completed"))) %>% 
  select(income_personal, education, pwgtp, pwgtp1:pwgtp80)

pair_t_design <- pair_t %>%
  as_survey_rep(
    weights    = pwgtp,
    repweights = all_of(rep_weights),
    type       = "successive-difference",
    mse        = TRUE
)
```

```{r}
# Get unique education levels (drop NAs)
edu_levels <- pair_t_design$variables$education |>
  unique() |>
  na.omit()

# Generate all pairwise combinations
pairs <- combn(edu_levels, 2, simplify = FALSE)

# Run svyttest for each pair
results <- map_dfr(pairs, function(pair) {
  sub_design <- subset(pair_t_design, education %in% pair)
  
  test <- svyttest(income_personal ~ education, design = sub_design)
  
  tibble(
    group1    = pair[1],
    group2    = pair[2],
    diff      = coef(test),          # mean(group1) - mean(group2)
    statistic = as.numeric(test$statistic),
    df        = as.numeric(test$parameter),
    p_value   = test$p.value,
    conf_low  = test$conf.int[1],
    conf_high = test$conf.int[2]
  )
})

# Adjust p-values for multiple comparisons
results <- results |>
  mutate(p_adj = p.adjust(p_value, method = "bonferroni"))  # or "bonferroni"

results
```

```{r}
table_pair_edu <- results %>%
  filter(p_adj > 0.05) %>%
  arrange(p_adj) %>%
  mutate(
    statistic = round(statistic, 3),
    p_value   = ifelse(p_value < 0.001, "< .001", round(p_value, 3)),
    p_adj     = ifelse(p_adj < 0.001, "< .001", round(p_adj, 3)),
    conf_low  = round(conf_low, 0),
    conf_high = round(conf_high, 0)
  ) %>%
  gt() %>%
  tab_header(title = "Table 3: Non-Significant Pairwise Comparisons of Income by Education") %>%
  cols_label(
    group1    = "Group 1",
    group2    = "Group 2",
    statistic = "t",
    df        = "df",
    p_value   = "p",
    conf_low  = "95% CI Low",
    conf_high = "95% CI High",
    p_adj     = "p adjusted"
  ) %>%
  tab_source_note("Pairs where Bonferroni-adjusted p > .05.")

gtsave(table_pair_edu,
       "../../statistical_report/table_pair_edu.png")
```

```{r}
summary_inc_edu <- pair_t %>%
  mutate(
  education = factor(education,
                     levels = c("Post-bacc professional degree",
                                "Doctorate degree",
                                "Master's degree",
                                "Bachelor's degree",
                                "Associate's degree",
                                "Some college",
                                "Regular high school diploma",
                                "GED or alternative credential",
                                "Less than High School",
                                "No schooling completed"))) %>%
  filter(!is.na(income_personal), !is.na(education)) %>%
  group_by(education) %>%
  summarise(
    n    = n(),
    mean = mean(income_personal, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd   = sd(income_personal, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(education) %>%
  gt(rowname_col = "education") %>%
  tab_stubhead(label = "Education") %>%
  tab_header(title = "Table 2: Income by Education Level") %>%
  cols_label(
    n    = "n",
    mean = "Mean Income ($)",
    sd   = "SD"
  ) %>%
  fmt_number(columns = c(n, mean, sd), decimals = 0)

gtsave(summary_inc_edu,
       "../../statistical_report/summary_inc_edu.png")
```
